name: Chocolate-Walnut Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 5 tablespoons Unsalted butter plus 1 teaspoon Unsalted butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Place the walnuts on a cookie sheet and toast until golden brown, about 10 minutes. Let cool and then chop coarsely.
3. In a double boiler over simmering water, melt the chocolate and butter together. Remove from the heat and stir until smooth. Let cool for 10 minutes.
4. Sift together the flour and baking powder.
5. In a large bowl, beat the eggs lightly. Gradually beat in the sugar. Add the orange zest.
6. Stir in the cooled chocolate mixture until blended. Stir in the sifted flour and baking powder until incorporated.
7. Fold in the chopped walnuts. Divide the dough in half and wrap each portion in plastic wrap. Refrigerate at least 1 hour or overnight.
8. Butter a large cookie sheet and preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
9. Shape each half of the dough into a log measuring approximately 14 x 2-1/2 inches. Place logs about 4 inches apart on the prepared pan. Smooth the tops and sides with a rubber spatula.
10. Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until the logs are firm when pressed in the center. Remove the baking sheet from the oven.
11. Slide the logs onto a cutting board. With a large knife, cut each log diagonally into 1/2-inch slices.
12. Stand the slices upright on edge on the prepared cookie sheet. Return to the oven and bake for an additional 15 minutes, or until crisp. Transfer to wire racks to cool completely.
